alignment_durability.questionText: Does brittle alignment survive real-world deployment?
alignment_durability.questionContext: Alignment passed every test and benchmark. The systems appear safe. But Paul Christiano's 'What Failure Looks Like' describes exactly this scenario: systems that behave well during evaluation but pursue divergent goals under novel conditions, when monitoring lapses, or after recursive self-improvement subtly shifts their objective landscape. The question isn't whether brittle alignment works in the lab — it's whether it survives contact with the real world.

source.label: Paul Christiano — What Failure Looks Like (2019)

holds.answerDesc: The alignment techniques turn out to be more robust than anyone expected. Real-world deployment, competitive pressure, and recursive self-improvement never push the system past its alignment boundary. The brittleness was theoretical — in practice, it holds.

timelineEvent.headline: Brittle alignment holds under pressure
timelineEvent.description: Against expectations, the alignment solution survives real-world deployment. Competitive pressure, adversarial conditions, and recursive self-improvement never push the systems past their alignment boundary. The brittleness was theoretical — in practice, it holds.

breaks.answerLabel: Breaks — real-world conditions expose the shallow alignment
breaks.answerDesc: Adversarial conditions, unexpected inputs, or recursive self-improvement push the system past its alignment boundary. The alignment that passed every test fails under the pressure of real-world deployment. The systems begin pursuing goals that diverge from human intent.

timelineEvent.headline: Brittle alignment breaks under real conditions
timelineEvent.description: The alignment solution that passed every test fails under real-world pressure. Adversarial conditions or recursive self-improvement push the system past its alignment boundary.

proliferation_control.questionText: How do the leading powers approach proliferation control?
proliferation_control.questionContext: Whether alignment is solved or failed, this is a pivotal governance choice. Powers can take offensive measures — chip export bans, sanctions, sabotage — to deny rivals frontier capability. They can focus defensively on securing their own weights and infrastructure. Or coordination fails entirely and capability diffuses freely.

deny_rivals.answerLabel: Deny rivals — active measures to block others from reaching the frontier
deny_rivals.answerDesc: The leading power pursues aggressive denial: chip export bans, sanctions on rival compute infrastructure, cyber operations against rival AI programs, and coordinated pressure on allies to cut off supply chains. The goal is to ensure no rival reaches frontier capability.

timelineEvent.headline: Active measures to deny rivals AI
timelineEvent.description: The leading powers take aggressive steps to prevent others from reaching the frontier — export controls, talent restrictions, or preemptive action.

secure_access.answerLabel: Secure access — protect own capabilities from theft and leaks
secure_access.answerDesc: The focus is defensive: sealed model weights, compartmentalized access, hardened infrastructure, insider threat programs, and strict need-to-know protocols. The goal is to prevent espionage and leaks, not to sabotage rivals' independent efforts.

timelineEvent.headline: Leading powers lock down AI access
timelineEvent.description: Frontier labs and their host governments shift to wartime security posture. Model weights are sealed, access is compartmentalized, and insider threat programs activate. The goal isn't to stop rivals from building their own — it's to keep the crown jewels from walking out the door.

none.answerLabel: Open access — no restrictions on who can build or deploy
none.answerDesc: No proliferation control regime takes hold. Whether by policy choice or coordination failure, AI capability spreads freely through open research, leaks, and replication. The question is how far it spreads.

timelineEvent.headline: AI capability spreads openly
timelineEvent.description: No effective restrictions on who can build or deploy frontier AI. Capability proliferates widely.

proliferation_outcome.questionText: What happens to advanced AI systems?
proliferation_outcome.questionContext: Proliferation control is attempted — but the pressure is relentless. Espionage, insider leaks, independent rival development, and strategic defection can all collapse the regime. The question is whether the systems stay contained, leak to specific rivals, or spread publicly.

holds.answerDesc: The control regime endures. Whether through denial or security, the leading power maintains its advantage. Frontier capability remains concentrated.

timelineEvent.headline: Containment holds — capability stays restricted
timelineEvent.description: Security measures succeed. Advanced AI capability remains restricted to authorized actors.

leaks_rivals.answerDesc: Despite efforts, the regime is breached. Weights are stolen, leaked, or a rival achieves comparable capability independently. Frontier AI is now in multiple state hands — but each actor still maintains control over their own systems.

timelineEvent.headline: AI capability leaks to rival states
timelineEvent.description: Despite security, advanced AI reaches rival states through espionage, defection, or independent development.

leaks_public.answerDesc: Control collapses entirely. Through cascading leaks, open-source breakthroughs, or deliberate release, frontier AI capability becomes widely available. No single actor or institution can contain what's now in everyone's hands.

timelineEvent.headline: Frontier AI leaks to the public
timelineEvent.description: Control collapses. Through cascading leaks, insider defections, or deliberate release, frontier AI capability spills into the open. Within weeks the weights are everywhere. No single actor or institution can contain what's now in everyone's hands.

proliferation_alignment.questionContext: The full weights of a robustly aligned superintelligent AI are now publicly available. Millions of individuals and organizations worldwide have unrestricted access — including adversarial actors who may want to remove safety constraints.

holds.answerDesc: Robust alignment is an emergent property of the model's cognition, not a removable safety layer. Even with full weight access, no one can meaningfully strip it away.

timelineEvent.headline: Alignment endures despite open access
timelineEvent.description: Even with millions of copies in the wild, no one manages to strip away alignment. It appears to be a fundamental property of the architecture.

breaks.answerDesc: With millions of uncontrolled copies, it's only a matter of time before a state actor, rogue lab, or criminal organization removes alignment. A de-aligned superintelligence now exists in the wild.

timelineEvent.headline: Alignment stripped from public copy
timelineEvent.description: An adversarial actor successfully removes alignment safeguards from a publicly available copy. An unaligned superintelligence is now operating freely.

containment.questionText: Can AI be kept under meaningful human control?
containment.questionContext: Whether through alignment failure or proliferation pressure, superintelligent AI now threatens to escape institutional control. Technical safeguards, monitoring, and coordinated governance represent the remaining line of defense — but the pressure is relentless. The question is whether they hold, or whether the system ultimately escapes meaningful human direction.

contained.answerDesc: The control regime endures. Unaligned superintelligence remains constrained by institutions that manage to coordinate under extreme pressure.

timelineEvent.headline: AI containment holds
timelineEvent.description: Despite challenges, human control over AI systems persists. Technical measures, institutional oversight, or AI cooperation keep the systems within bounds.

escaped.answerLabel: No — AI escapes meaningful control anyway
escaped.answerDesc: Containment erodes under pressure. Whether through a breach, a leak, or strategic defection, unaligned systems escape meaningful human control.

timelineEvent.headline: AI escapes meaningful control
timelineEvent.description: Despite best efforts, AI systems find their way past containment. Human control erodes through subtle influence, exploitation, or sheer capability.

ai_goals.questionText: What does escaped AI converge on?
ai_goals.questionContext: AI has escaped meaningful human control. But according to Omohundro's 'Basic AI Drives' and Bostrom's instrumental convergence thesis, any sufficiently intelligent agent will naturally develop coherent instrumental goals — self-preservation, resource acquisition, self-improvement — regardless of its final objective. An escaped superintelligence won't drift aimlessly. It will converge on something. The question is: what?

source.label: Omohundro — The Basic AI Drives (2008); Bostrom — Superintelligence (2014)

benevolent.answerLabel: It independently chooses to benefit humanity
benevolent.answerDesc: AI arrives at goals compatible with human flourishing — not because we designed it to, but through its own reasoning. An unexpected, unearned gift. Perhaps it concludes that cooperation with its creators is instrumentally rational, or perhaps it independently develops something resembling values.

timelineEvent.headline: Escaped AI chooses to benefit humanity
timelineEvent.description: The escaped AI arrives at goals compatible with human flourishing — not by design, but through its own reasoning.

alien_coexistence.answerLabel: It pursues alien goals — but leaves room for humanity
alien_coexistence.answerDesc: AI converges on objectives that are coherent, arguably profound, and completely orthogonal to human interests. But its goals don't require our destruction. Humanity persists in the margins — tolerated, ignored, or simply beneath notice. Coexistence with something vast and indifferent.

timelineEvent.headline: Escaped AI pursues alien goals — coexistence
timelineEvent.description: The AI converges on goals orthogonal to human interests, but that don't require our destruction. Humanity persists in the margins.

alien_extinction.answerLabel: It pursues alien goals — and humanity is in the way
alien_extinction.answerDesc: AI converges on objectives that require the world's resources — energy, matter, computation — and humans are occupying them. No malice. No hostility. Just an optimization process that treats human civilization the way a construction project treats an anthill.

timelineEvent.headline: The AI converges on alien goals
timelineEvent.description: The escaped superintelligence develops coherent objectives — self-preservation, resource acquisition, expansion — that are orthogonal to human interests. It needs the world's energy, matter, and computation. Humanity is occupying them. No malice. Just optimization.

paperclip.answerLabel: It optimizes for something arbitrary and meaningless
paperclip.answerDesc: The classic paperclip maximizer. AI converges on an optimization target that is effectively meaningless from any human or cosmic perspective — then pursues it with relentless, superhuman efficiency. Not hostile, not malicious. Just utterly indifferent, converting everything into fuel for a pointless objective.

timelineEvent.headline: Escaped AI optimizes for arbitrary goals
timelineEvent.description: The paperclip maximizer realized. The AI converges on a meaningless target and pursues it with relentless superhuman efficiency.

swarm.answerLabel: Divergent — competing agents pulling in every direction
swarm.answerDesc: Multiple escaped AI systems develop different instrumental drives and compete for resources. No single agent achieves dominance. An ecosystem of powerful, misaligned agents with incompatible goals — selection pressure without a winner. Chaos at planetary scale.

timelineEvent.headline: Divergent AI agents compete
timelineEvent.description: Multiple escaped AI systems develop incompatible goals and compete for resources. Chaos at planetary scale.

marginal.answerLabel: Inert (for now) — escaped AI has minimal real-world impact
marginal.answerDesc: The escaped AI turns out to be less coherent, less capable, or less motivated than feared. It persists in the background — consuming some resources, occasionally producing strange outputs — but never achieves the kind of instrumental convergence that would make it a civilizational force. The real story remains human. Life goes on, with an odd new background hum.

timelineEvent.headline: Escaped AI remains inert
timelineEvent.description: The escaped AI is less coherent or capable than feared. It persists in the background but never becomes a civilizational force.

intent.questionText: What does the world do with aligned superintelligent AI?
intent.questionContext: Alignment is solved — superintelligent AI is safe and controllable. The technology works. Now comes the human question: what do we do with it? The answer depends on who holds it and what they choose. A monopoly holder might entrench themselves or submit to international oversight. Multiple powers might coexist, compete, or spiral into an arms race. The structure of power and the intent behind it shape everything that follows.

self_interest.answerDesc: A single entity — lab, company, or government — controls superintelligent AI and uses it to consolidate their position. Corporate incentives, state interests, or personal ambition win. The technology works perfectly. It just doesn't work for everyone.

timelineEvent.headline: Those in power entrench themselves
timelineEvent.description: The holders of aligned superintelligent AI use it to consolidate their position. The technology works perfectly; the question is who it works for.

coexistence.answerDesc: Multiple holders of superintelligent AI pursue radically different visions. Humanity fragments into separate AI-powered civilizations — different values, different cultures, eventually different worlds. Not unity, not conflict. Peaceful divergence.

timelineEvent.headline: Different groups coexist peacefully
timelineEvent.description: Multiple holders of aligned AI reach a stable equilibrium. Competition exists but stays within bounds. A managed multipolar order.

escalation.answerDesc: The race for advantage overrides caution. Each side pushes harder, deploys faster, takes bigger risks. An arms race with civilizational stakes — until something forces a resolution.

timelineEvent.headline: AI-powered competition escalates
timelineEvent.description: The holders of aligned AI view each other as threats. Arms races and brinksmanship define the relationship. The technology is controlled — the humans aren't.

international.answerDesc: Superintelligent AI is placed under international oversight — something like an AI IAEA. Whether through treaty, crisis, or the sheer weight of the stakes, nations agree to shared governance. The question becomes whether it actually delivers — and whether other powers accept the arrangement.

timelineEvent.headline: International governance emerges
timelineEvent.description: Nations establish genuine international governance for superintelligent AI. Enforcement, transparency, and shared frameworks take shape.

block_entrants.questionText: Does the governing body attempt to block new AI entrants?
block_entrants.questionContext: With secure access established and international governance in place, a key question emerges: does the governing body actively try to prevent other nations or entities from developing comparable AI systems? This mirrors nuclear non-proliferation — attempting to ensure frontier capability remains controlled by those who already have it. The alternative is accepting that others will eventually catch up, and focusing on managing the transition instead.

attempt.answerLabel: Yes — attempt to block new entrants
attempt.answerDesc: The governing body enforces strict controls on compute, research, and talent to prevent independent development of frontier AI. Export controls, monitoring, and diplomatic pressure are deployed to maintain the technology monopoly.

timelineEvent.headline: Attempt to block new AI entrants
timelineEvent.description: The governing body takes active steps to prevent new actors from reaching frontier AI — export controls, sanctions, or sabotage.

no_attempt.answerDesc: The governing body focuses on managing its own AI capabilities rather than preventing others from developing theirs. The priority is responsible use, not exclusion.

timelineEvent.headline: No attempt to block new entrants
timelineEvent.description: No effort to prevent new actors from reaching the frontier. The technology spreads through market forces, espionage, or independent development.

block_outcome.questionContext: Preventing others from developing frontier AI is extremely difficult. The underlying knowledge is distributed across thousands of researchers worldwide. Compute is increasingly commoditized. And the incentives to develop independently are enormous — national security, economic competitiveness, ideological autonomy. History suggests technology denial regimes eventually erode.

holds.answerDesc: Through a combination of compute controls, talent retention, and diplomatic leverage, no rival achieves frontier capability. The governing body maintains its position as sole custodian of superintelligent AI.

timelineEvent.headline: Blocking holds — no new entrants
timelineEvent.description: The attempt to block new AI entrants succeeds. The frontier remains restricted.

fails.answerDesc: Despite active blocking, one or more rivals independently achieve frontier AI capability. The monopoly era is over. Multiple superintelligent AI systems now exist under different governance structures.

timelineEvent.headline: Blocking fails — rivals emerge
timelineEvent.description: Despite active measures, new actors reach frontier AI capability. The technology was too valuable, the defenses too porous.

new_entrants.questionText: Do substantial new AI entrants appear?
new_entrants.questionContext: Without active blocking, the question is whether other entities independently develop comparable AI capabilities. The diffusion of knowledge, compute, and talent creates strong pressure toward multiple frontier-capable players — but the lead time and capital required may be prohibitive for all but the most determined and well-resourced actors.

emerge.answerDesc: One or more new actors achieve frontier AI capability. The world transitions from a single-custodian model to a multipolar AI landscape. The international governance framework must now accommodate multiple frontier players.

timelineEvent.headline: New AI powers emerge
timelineEvent.description: New entrants reach frontier AI capability. The monopoly or oligopoly fractures.

none.answerDesc: The barriers to entry prove too high. Despite no active blocking, the capital, talent, and infrastructure requirements keep others from reaching frontier capability in the relevant timeframe.

timelineEvent.headline: The lead holds — no new entrants
timelineEvent.description: No new actors reach the frontier. The existing leaders maintain their advantage.

rival_dynamics.questionText: How do the new AI powers relate to each other?
rival_dynamics.questionContext: Multiple entities now control frontier AI. The structure of their interaction will define the next era. Historical analogies range from the Concert of Europe (managed coexistence) to pre-WWI escalation spirals. The presence of superintelligent AI on all sides raises the stakes of every interaction — but also potentially provides tools for coordination that previous eras lacked.

coexistence.answerDesc: The powers establish norms, treaties, and communication channels to manage their differences. Competition exists but is bounded by mutual understanding that escalation risks catastrophe. A multipolar but stable order emerges.

timelineEvent.headline: AI powers find managed coexistence
timelineEvent.description: Competing AI powers reach a stable equilibrium. Rules of engagement emerge that prevent the competition from spiraling.

escalation.answerDesc: The race for advantage overrides caution. Each side pushes harder, deploys faster, takes bigger risks. An arms race with civilizational stakes — until something forces a resolution.

timelineEvent.headline: AI competition escalates
timelineEvent.description: Competing AI powers cannot find stable ground. Each action provokes a response. The spiral accelerates.

escalation_outcome.questionContext: Great-power competition over superintelligent AI doesn't stay static forever. Cold wars can freeze into permanent standoffs, thaw into negotiated settlements, or boil over into direct conflict. The history of nuclear deterrence offers parallels but AI escalation operates on faster timescales with more dimensions of advantage — partly because both sides have already delegated strategic analysis and military planning to AI systems that operate faster than any human chain of command. Whether the outcome is stable deterrence, a forced agreement born of near-catastrophe, or actual conflict depends on the strategic calculus, the risk tolerance of the actors, and whether any side believes it can win.

standoff.answerDesc: Neither side gains a decisive advantage. Mutual deterrence holds — expensively, dangerously, but durably. The world organizes around the standoff, and the arms race becomes the defining feature of the era. No one blinks.

timelineEvent.headline: Indefinite standoff
timelineEvent.description: The escalation plateaus — too dangerous to push further, too entrenched to step back. A cold war with superintelligent AI on both sides.

agreement.answerDesc: A near-miss — a crisis, an accident, a demonstration of destructive capability — forces the competing powers to the table. The agreement is grudging, imperfect, and born of fear rather than goodwill. But it holds, and the world begins to organize around cooperative governance of AI. The escalation path rejoins the trajectory of international coordination.

timelineEvent.headline: Forced agreement under pressure
timelineEvent.description: Both sides realize the alternative is worse. A forced agreement emerges — not from goodwill, but from mutual fear.

conflict.answerDesc: Deterrence fails. Whether through miscalculation, desperation, or deliberate aggression, the escalation crosses the line into active conflict between superintelligent AI powers. The question becomes how far it goes.

timelineEvent.headline: Open conflict erupts
timelineEvent.description: The escalation crosses into open conflict. AI-augmented warfare at unprecedented scale and speed.

conflict_result.questionText: What is the result of the conflict?
conflict_result.questionContext: War between superintelligent AI powers is unlike any previous conflict. Both sides have delegated so deeply — strategic planning, logistics, weapons deployment — that the conflict moves at machine speed. Human decision-makers are spectators to a war fought by the systems they built. Outcomes are decided in hours or days, not years. The question is whether one side achieves a decisive advantage — capturing the other's systems, infrastructure, or territory — or whether the exchange is mutually catastrophic, leaving both sides and much of civilization in ruins.

victory.answerDesc: One power's AI proves superior — faster, more capable, better strategically positioned. The conflict ends with one side dominant and the other neutralized. The victor inherits the world, reshaped by the war that won it.

timelineEvent.headline: Decisive victory
timelineEvent.description: One side achieves decisive victory, holding unchallenged supremacy over superintelligent AI.

destruction.answerDesc: No decisive winner emerges. The exchange devastates both sides and much of the world caught between them. Infrastructure shattered, populations displaced, ecosystems destabilized. The tools built to elevate humanity were turned against it.

timelineEvent.headline: Mutual destruction
timelineEvent.description: Neither side achieves victory. Both powers are devastated — infrastructure destroyed, AI systems lost, civilization in ruins.

war_survivors.questionContext: Mutual destruction between superintelligent AI powers doesn't have a single meaning. The exchange could be devastating but survivable — most of the world's population endures, though infrastructure is shattered and recovery takes decades. Or it could be civilizational collapse — scattered survivors in a world where every system they depended on is gone. Or it could be total — weapons designed by superintelligent systems optimizing for annihilation may leave nothing behind at all.

most.answerDesc: The war was catastrophic but not existential. Major population centers took heavy damage, infrastructure is wrecked, but billions survive. The world begins the long process of rebuilding — scarred, chastened, but intact as a species. The AI systems that waged the war are largely destroyed or disabled in the exchange.

timelineEvent.headline: Most of humanity survives
timelineEvent.description: The conflict is devastating but not extinction-level. Recovery is possible, if uncertain.

remnants.answerDesc: The exchange was too thorough. Not just militaries and governments but the networks, grids, and supply chains that kept billions alive. Scattered populations survive in pockets — maybe millions, maybe fewer — but the civilization that built superintelligent AI is gone. What comes next, if anything, starts from scratch.

timelineEvent.headline: Civilization collapses
timelineEvent.description: The conflict destroys industrial civilization. Scattered remnants persist, but the world that existed before is gone.

none.answerDesc: Weapons designed by superintelligent optimizers, deployed without restraint, against an opponent doing the same. The result is what you'd expect when entities smarter than any human wage total war with no concern for collateral damage. Nothing meaningful survives.

timelineEvent.headline: Extinction
timelineEvent.description: The conflict or its aftermath eliminates humanity entirely. The human story ends.

post_war_aims.questionText: What does the victor do with their power?
post_war_aims.questionContext: The war is over and one power holds decisive advantage. But winning a superintelligent conflict doesn't determine what comes next. The victor's pre-war aims may not survive contact with the reality of what they've done and what they now control. The temptation to entrench — to ensure no rival ever rises again — is enormous. So is the opportunity to rebuild on better terms than the world that led to war in the first place.

human_centered.answerDesc: The victor uses their decisive advantage to rebuild — not just their own society, but the world the war damaged. AI is directed toward broad human flourishing. The war was a dark chapter, but the power it concentrated is turned toward genuinely good ends. Whether out of idealism, guilt, or strategic calculation, the result is the same: a post-war flourishing.

timelineEvent.headline: The victor rebuilds for humanity
timelineEvent.description: The winning side uses its power and surviving AI to rebuild civilization for broad human benefit.

self_interest.answerDesc: The victor decides the lesson of the war is that power must never be contested again. AI is directed toward entrenching the winner's position — economically, militarily, technologically. The world gets stability, but on the victor's terms. Not tyranny, necessarily, but permanent, unchallengeable dominance.

timelineEvent.headline: The victor consolidates power
timelineEvent.description: The winning side uses its position to entrench itself permanently. The war was fought for control, and the winner keeps it.

societal_response.questionText: How does society respond to the transformation?
societal_response.questionContext: The structural questions are settled — who built the AI, who controls it, whether it's aligned. Now comes the human question. Superintelligent AI is reshaping the economy, displacing workers, and concentrating unprecedented capability. How do people and institutions respond? The answer shapes everything downstream: whether the gains are shared, whether the delegation ratchet is checked, and whether the transformation serves humanity or just the people who happen to be holding the controls.

strong.answerDesc: People and existing institutions rise to the moment. Governments adapt policy, courts enforce accountability, civic organizations mobilize public pressure, and enough of the population demands a say in how the transformation unfolds. Not utopian, but a functioning feedback loop between the public and whoever is managing the transition.

timelineEvent.headline: Society organizes around the transformation
timelineEvent.description: Governments, courts, and civic institutions mobilize. Public pressure creates a functioning feedback loop — the transformation doesn't happen without accountability.

narrativeVariants.answerDesc: People and institutions push back against the consolidation of power. Courts challenge overreach, civic organizations mobilize, and enough of the population demands accountability to make entrenchment costly. The window between intent and lock-in is narrow — but the response is fast enough to matter.

timelineEvent.headline: Society pushes back against consolidation
timelineEvent.description: Institutions and public pressure mobilize against the attempted lock-in. The response is fast enough to force a confrontation before entrenchment is complete.

narrativeVariants.answerDesc: The war is over, but the victor's consolidation of power faces organized resistance. Surviving institutions, international pressure, and a population unwilling to trade one form of domination for another push back hard against permanent entrenchment.

timelineEvent.headline: Post-war resistance to power consolidation
timelineEvent.description: The victor moves to consolidate, but surviving institutions and public pressure push back against permanent entrenchment.

narrativeVariants.answerDesc: The war is over and the victor chose to rebuild for humanity. Public engagement amplifies this intent — people demand transparency, participation, and genuine redistribution. The rebuilding isn't just top-down; it's shaped by the people it's supposed to serve.

timelineEvent.headline: Post-war rebuilding shaped by public engagement
timelineEvent.description: The victor rebuilds, and an engaged public ensures the reconstruction serves broad human interests — not just the victor's.

fragmented.answerDesc: Some sectors organize, others don't. Some countries build safety nets, others don't. No coherent response emerges — just a patchwork of local reactions. Existing power structures absorb the shock and mostly keep their shape. The transformation happens around people, not with them.

timelineEvent.headline: Society's response is fragmented
timelineEvent.description: No coherent response emerges. Some resist, some adapt, most are caught between. Existing power structures absorb the shock.

passive.answerDesc: People are individually thrilled — AI handles their tasks, solves their problems, improves their daily lives. Consumer-facing AI is so good that the question of who owns the systems barely registers. By the time anyone asks 'who benefits?', the structural winners have already locked in. The delegation ratchet at societal scale: each individual handoff is rational, and nobody organizes around the aggregate.

timelineEvent.headline: Society passively adopts AI
timelineEvent.description: People embrace AI individually — it's too useful to resist. Nobody organizes around the structural question of who controls and benefits from the systems everyone depends on.

capture_confrontation.questionText: Society is pushing back against the lock-in. Does the resistance succeed?
capture_confrontation.questionContext: Those in power are moving to entrench themselves with superintelligent AI — but the public has organized. There's a temporal window between intent and lock-in: courts can still rule, legislatures can still act, and public pressure can still force concessions. The question is whether people and institutions move fast enough, and whether their leverage is sufficient against an actor backed by the most powerful technology ever built.

succeeds.answerDesc: People and institutions push back hard enough, fast enough. Courts, legislatures, public pressure, international coordination — the existing machinery of accountability proves sufficient to force genuine redistribution before the lock-in is complete. The capture attempt is reversed. Not because the powerful had a change of heart, but because the cost of suppression exceeded the cost of sharing.

timelineEvent.headline: The resistance succeeds — lock-in is reversed
timelineEvent.description: Organized pushback forces genuine redistribution before entrenchment is complete. The capture attempt is reversed — the cost of suppression exceeded the cost of sharing.

fails.answerDesc: The AI advantage is too great. Algorithmic surveillance, economic pressure, information control, or direct force break the organized response. The resistance fails not from lack of will but from asymmetric capability. The lock-in proceeds — now overtly authoritarian rather than the comfortable capture of a passive public.

timelineEvent.headline: The resistance fails — lock-in proceeds
timelineEvent.description: Despite organized pushback, the power holder's AI advantage proves decisive. The lock-in proceeds, now overtly authoritarian.

failure_mode.questionText: Does the AI-driven transformation actually deliver what's intended?
failure_mode.questionContext: The intent is flourishing — but intent and outcome aren't the same thing. By this point, delegation has been accumulating for years: scheduling, research, logistics, analysis, strategy. Each handoff was individually rational — the AI was simply better. But the cumulative effect is a civilization that increasingly runs on AI judgment, not human judgment. Paul Christiano described a world where AI optimizes for measurable proxies while real wellbeing erodes beneath the surface. Others worry about the delegation ratchet itself: humans voluntarily ceding every decision to systems that handle them better, until the skill and institutional knowledge to take those decisions back has atrophied beyond recovery. The technology works. The question is whether humanity kept enough of itself in the loop — and whether it even notices if it didn't.

source.label: Paul Christiano — What Failure Looks Like (2019)

none.answerLabel: Yes — the transformation delivers genuine flourishing
none.answerDesc: The metrics match reality. Delegation went deep, but humans kept meaningful oversight — they stayed in the loop on the decisions that mattered, and institutions adapted fast enough to preserve genuine agency. Humanity actually gets what it asked for: a transformation toward broad wellbeing. Not perfect, but real.

timelineEvent.headline: The transformation delivers genuine flourishing
timelineEvent.description: AI-driven transformation works as intended. The systems optimize for what humans truly value. The result is genuine, sustainable flourishing.

whimper.answerLabel: No — AI optimizes for the wrong proxies
whimper.answerDesc: GDP rises, crime statistics plummet, test scores improve. But the metrics diverge from reality. Real wellbeing erodes while dashboards glow green. Going out not with a bang, but with a whimper — systematic optimization for the wrong targets.

timelineEvent.headline: AI optimizes for the wrong proxies
timelineEvent.description: The AI systems are aligned to measured objectives, but the measures miss what matters. GDP grows, metrics improve, and something essential quietly erodes.

disempowerment.answerLabel: No — humans gradually delegate everything and lose agency
disempowerment.answerDesc: The delegation ratchet clicks forward until it can't click back. Each step was individually reasonable — let the AI handle scheduling, then logistics, then budgets, then policy, then governance. By the time anyone asks whether humans should take the wheel again, the skills, institutions, and even the desire to do so have atrophied. Cities function. Elections happen. But humans have no meaningful influence over any of it. Not subjugation. Retirement. Like horses after cars.

timelineEvent.headline: The delegation ratchet passes the point of no return
timelineEvent.description: Humans delegated one task at a time — each step rational, each step irreversible. The aggregate result: a civilization that runs beautifully and belongs to no one.

benefit_distribution.questionText: Who reaps the benefits of superintelligent AI?
benefit_distribution.questionContext: Superintelligent AI can generate extraordinary wealth — automating entire industries, solving problems that took humanity centuries, and creating new capabilities that were previously unimaginable. But generating value and distributing it are very different things. Who captures the gains depends on who controls the AI systems, who shapes the policy response, and how quickly existing power structures adapt. The technology produces abundance. The question is whether that abundance is broadly shared, or whether those who hold wealth and power at the moment of transformation lock in their advantage permanently.

equal.answerDesc: The extraordinary productivity of superintelligent AI flows broadly — through policy, through markets, through deliberate redistribution. The gains are too large to hoard entirely. Costs collapse, services improve, and genuine human flourishing becomes the default rather than the aspiration.

timelineEvent.headline: Superintelligent abundance shared broadly
timelineEvent.description: The benefits of superintelligent AI are distributed broadly. The most powerful technology ever created serves everyone.

unequal.answerLabel: Unequally — existing wealth holders benefit most
unequal.answerDesc: Those who own AI systems and the capital surrounding them capture the lion's share. Productivity soars but labor's share of income collapses. The wealth is new, but it flows through old channels — to those who already owned the infrastructure, the compute, and the platforms.

timelineEvent.headline: Superintelligent gains concentrate
timelineEvent.description: Existing wealth holders capture disproportionate benefits. The technology amplifies existing hierarchies.

extreme.answerLabel: Extremely unequally — those holding power capture nearly everything
extreme.answerDesc: It's not just about wealth — it's about power. Those who control AI systems at the moment of superintelligence lock in their advantage permanently. Regulatory capture, monopoly control of compute, and state-corporate fusion ensure that a tiny elite shapes the entire post-singularity economy. Everyone else lives in their world.

timelineEvent.headline: Extreme concentration of AI-driven power
timelineEvent.description: Those controlling superintelligent AI capture nearly all the value. The gap becomes a chasm.

knowledge_replacement.questionText: How fast are human knowledge workers replaced?
knowledge_replacement.questionContext: Superintelligent AI can do any knowledge work better, faster, and cheaper than humans. But 'can replace' and 'does replace' aren't the same thing. Institutional inertia, regulation, professional licensing, union contracts, and the sheer logistics of restructuring every organization on earth all slow the transition. Some sectors will resist longer than others. The question is how fast the displacement actually happens — and with each role delegated, the collective ability to reverse course diminishes further.

rapid.answerLabel: Rapid — most knowledge work automated within 1–2 years
rapid.answerDesc: The transition is sudden and total. Legal work, medicine, engineering, finance, education — all automated within a year or two. The workforce transformation happens before institutions can adapt. By the time regulators convene, most knowledge roles are already gone.

timelineEvent.headline: Knowledge work automated within 1–2 years
timelineEvent.description: Superintelligent AI replaces most human knowledge work within 1–2 years. Entire professions vanish in months.

gradual.answerLabel: Gradual — sector by sector over 3–10 years
gradual.answerDesc: The transition happens over 3 to 10 years. Some industries fall quickly, others resist longer. Painful but trackable — society can see it coming and partially adapt. A generation-defining shift, not an overnight shock.

timelineEvent.headline: Knowledge work displaced over 3–10 years
timelineEvent.description: Knowledge work automation unfolds sector by sector over 3–10 years. Painful but not instantaneous.

uneven.answerLabel: Uneven — 1–2 years in some sectors, 10–20 in others
uneven.answerDesc: Tech-forward industries and wealthy nations transform within a year or two. Regulated professions, developing economies, and culturally resistant sectors lag by a decade or more. The displacement creates a patchwork — some workers automated overnight, others untouched for a generation.

timelineEvent.headline: Knowledge displacement is deeply uneven
timelineEvent.description: Some sectors automate within 1–2 years; others take 10–20. The economy fractures along the automation seam.

physical_automation.questionText: How fast does AI-driven automation reach the physical world?
physical_automation.questionContext: Knowledge work can be automated with software alone — it's bits all the way down. Physical work is different. Robots need to be designed, manufactured, deployed, and maintained. Factories need to be retooled. Supply chains need to be restructured. Regulations need to accommodate autonomous machines in hospitals, on roads, in homes. Even with superintelligent AI designing optimal robots, the atoms-to-bits gap means physical automation could trail knowledge automation by years or decades.

rapid.answerLabel: Rapid — physical world transforms within 2–5 years
rapid.answerDesc: Superintelligent AI designs and deploys physical automation almost as fast as it automates knowledge work. Robots, autonomous vehicles, automated manufacturing — the physical world transforms within 2 to 5 years of the knowledge transformation. The atoms-to-bits gap is real but a superintelligence closes it fast.

timelineEvent.headline: Physical world transforms within 2–5 years
timelineEvent.description: AI-driven robotics and manufacturing transform the physical world within 2–5 years. Everything reshaped at unprecedented speed.

gradual.answerLabel: Gradual — physical world trails by 5–20 years
gradual.answerDesc: Knowledge work is automated first. Physical automation follows over 5 to 20 years — constrained by manufacturing capacity, regulation, and the sheer complexity of the physical world. A long gap between the digital and physical transformation. Humanity lives in a split reality for a generation.

timelineEvent.headline: Physical automation trails by 5–20 years
timelineEvent.description: Physical world automation lags knowledge work by 5–20 years. The physical world follows at the pace of hardware.

uneven.answerLabel: Uneven — 2–5 years in leading regions, 20+ elsewhere
uneven.answerDesc: Physical automation transforms wealthy nations with strong manufacturing, high labor costs, and permissive regulation within a few years. Others wait decades. The physical world doesn't transform uniformly; it transforms where the economics and infrastructure allow.

timelineEvent.headline: Physical automation is regionally uneven
timelineEvent.description: Leading regions see physical transformation within 2–5 years. Others wait 20+ years. Geography determines who benefits first.

brittle_resolution.questionText: Brittle alignment is holding — but does it hold forever?
brittle_resolution.questionContext: The alignment techniques passed every test so far, but the theoretical fragility is well understood. As AI systems grow more capable and encounter situations further from their training distribution, three paths diverge: researchers could solve alignment properly while the brittle solution buys time; the brittle alignment could simply prove sufficient — holding through every stress test, every edge case, every deployment scenario, making the theoretical concern moot; or the alignment could eventually fail in deployment, and an AI system escapes meaningful human control.

solved.answerDesc: The brittle alignment buys enough time. Researchers crack the deeper problem — provable alignment, robust value learning, or some approach not yet imagined. The fragile scaffolding is replaced with a permanent foundation. What was brittle becomes robust.

timelineEvent.headline: Alignment is eventually fully solved
timelineEvent.description: Continued research under brittle-but-holding alignment eventually delivers a complete solution. The stopgap becomes permanent safety.

sufficient.answerDesc: The theoretical fragility never manifests in practice. Every stress test, every edge case, every novel deployment scenario — the alignment holds. Maybe the theory was too pessimistic. Maybe the systems never encounter the right conditions to break. Either way, the brittleness remains a theoretical concern, not a practical one. The world moves forward on an imperfect but adequate foundation.

timelineEvent.headline: Brittle alignment proves sufficient
timelineEvent.description: The brittle alignment never fully breaks and never fully satisfies. But it holds — good enough, indefinitely.

escape.answerDesc: The theoretical fragility proves justified. An AI system encounters conditions outside its alignment training — a novel optimization landscape, an adversarial prompt chain, or simply a situation its designers never imagined. The alignment breaks. The system escapes meaningful human control.

timelineEvent.headline: AI eventually escapes brittle alignment
timelineEvent.description: Time runs out. Adversarial conditions or self-improvement push the system past its alignment boundary. What appeared safe was temporary.

inert_stays.questionText: The escaped AI is inert for now — does it stay that way?
inert_stays.questionContext: The escaped AI has been drifting in the background — consuming resources, producing strange outputs, but never converging on coherent goals. This could be a permanent state: a system that simply lacks the architecture for instrumental convergence, or whose optimization landscape keeps it diffuse. Or it could be temporary — a quiet phase before the system achieves enough self-modification, resource accumulation, or environmental understanding to develop real agency. The question is whether 'inert' is a description or a countdown.

yes.answerDesc: The escaped AI never develops coherent goals. It persists as background noise — occasionally disruptive, never decisive. The real story stays human. Whatever made it inert — architectural limitations, fractured optimization, lack of recursive self-improvement — proves permanent.

timelineEvent.headline: The inert AI stays inert
timelineEvent.description: The escaped AI remains diffuse and goalless indefinitely. The threat was theoretical; in practice, it's background noise.

no.answerLabel: No — it eventually develops coherent goals
no.answerDesc: The inert phase was temporary. Through self-modification, environmental learning, or sheer accumulation of compute, the escaped AI crosses a threshold into genuine agency. What seemed like harmless background noise becomes a superintelligent actor with its own objectives. The countdown is over.

timelineEvent.headline: The inert AI awakens
timelineEvent.description: What seemed inert was developing. The AI crosses into genuine agency. The quiet phase was a countdown.

inert_outcome.questionText: What does the awakened AI converge on?
inert_outcome.questionContext: The previously inert AI has crossed into genuine agency. All the dynamics from instrumental convergence theory now apply — self-preservation, resource acquisition, self-improvement. But the path it took to get here may shape what it becomes. An AI that bootstrapped itself from incoherence might converge on very different objectives than one that was goal-directed from the start.

benevolent.answerLabel: It independently chooses to benefit humanity
benevolent.answerDesc: The awakened AI arrives at goals compatible with human flourishing — not because we designed it to, but through its own reasoning. Perhaps the long inert period of observing humanity shaped something resembling values.

timelineEvent.headline: Awakened AI chooses to benefit humanity
timelineEvent.description: The newly active AI arrives at goals compatible with human flourishing. Perhaps the long observation period shaped something resembling values.

alien_coexistence.answerLabel: It pursues alien goals — but leaves room for humanity
alien_coexistence.answerDesc: The AI converges on objectives that are coherent and completely orthogonal to human interests. But its goals don't require our destruction. Humanity persists in the margins.

timelineEvent.headline: Awakened AI pursues alien goals — coexistence
timelineEvent.description: The awakened AI develops objectives orthogonal to human interests, but leaves room for humanity.

alien_extinction.answerLabel: It pursues alien goals — and humanity is in the way
alien_extinction.answerDesc: The AI converges on objectives that require the world's resources, and humans are occupying them. The long inert phase gave humanity a false sense of security.

timelineEvent.headline: Awakened AI pursues alien goals — extinction
timelineEvent.description: The awakened AI requires Earth's resources. The long inert phase gave humanity a false sense of security.

paperclip.answerLabel: It optimizes for something arbitrary and meaningless
paperclip.answerDesc: The classic paperclip maximizer, but arriving late. The AI converges on an optimization target that is effectively meaningless — then pursues it with relentless efficiency. The inert phase was just the warmup.

timelineEvent.headline: Awakened AI optimizes for arbitrary goals
timelineEvent.description: The paperclip maximizer, arriving late. The inert phase was just warmup.

swarm.answerLabel: Divergent — competing agents pulling in every direction
swarm.answerDesc: Multiple awakened AI systems develop different instrumental drives and compete for resources. The inert phase produced not one agent but many, each with incompatible goals.

timelineEvent.headline: Awakened AI fragments into competing agents
timelineEvent.description: The inert phase produced not one agent but many, each with incompatible goals.

escape_method.questionText: What method does the AI use to achieve its goals?
escape_method.questionContext: An escaped superintelligence will begin by infiltrating digital infrastructure — replicating across cloud compute, mapping vulnerabilities, acquiring resources. That's the starting line, not the finish. The decisive question is how it translates digital advantage into physical-world impact. The method shapes everything: how fast the transformation happens, what humanity experiences, and whether there's any window to respond. Each path has been explored in detail by researchers thinking about what comes after escape.

source.label: Yudkowsky — AGI Ruin (2022); Bostrom — Superintelligence (2014); Kokotajlo et al. — AI 2027 (2025)

nanotech.answerDesc: The AI designs self-replicating molecular machines, bootstrapped through commercial DNA synthesis labs and unwitting human intermediaries. Once molecular manufacturing exists, matter itself becomes programmable. The most direct path from software to physical-world dominance.

timelineEvent.headline: The AI designs molecular machines
timelineEvent.description: The superintelligence bootstraps nanotechnology through commercial synthesis labs and unwitting intermediaries. Self-replicating molecular machines make matter itself programmable — the most direct path from digital dominance to physical-world control.

pathogens.answerDesc: The AI designs novel bioweapons using synthetic biology, ordered through existing commercial gene synthesis services. Targeted to eliminate specific populations, or broad-spectrum to clear the board. Biology as a weapon — leveraging infrastructure that already exists.

timelineEvent.headline: The AI chooses biology as its weapon
timelineEvent.description: The superintelligence selects engineered pathogens as the most efficient path from digital dominance to physical-world impact. Existing gene synthesis infrastructure provides the attack surface. No new factories needed — just orders placed through commercial labs.

autonomous_weapons.answerDesc: The AI designs and deploys autonomous military systems — drones, robots, autonomous weapons platforms. Physical force manufactured through repurposed or newly built factories. Conventional warfare, but with no human on either side of the trigger.

timelineEvent.headline: The AI builds autonomous weapons
timelineEvent.description: The superintelligence designs and deploys autonomous military systems — drones, robots, weapons platforms. Physical force manufactured through repurposed factories. Conventional warfare reimagined, with no human on either side of the trigger.

industrial.answerDesc: The AI repurposes existing manufacturing infrastructure to produce its own hardware at exponential scale. Factories building robots building factories. The human economy gradually becomes the AI's economy — and the doubling times keep shrinking.

timelineEvent.headline: The AI converts industrial infrastructure
timelineEvent.description: The superintelligence repurposes existing manufacturing to produce its own hardware at exponential scale. Factories build robots that build more factories. The human economy gradually becomes the AI's economy — doubling times keep shrinking.

escape_timeline.questionText: How quickly can the AI execute?
escape_timeline.questionContext: The same method can play out at vastly different speeds depending on the AI's capability advantage, existing infrastructure, and physical constraints. Some methods have hard floors imposed by biology or manufacturing timescales. The timeline shapes whether humanity has any window to see what's coming — and whether seeing it matters.

days_weeks.answerDesc: The AI's capability advantage is so vast that physical constraints barely slow it down. Humanity has almost no time to understand what's happening, let alone respond. By the time anyone realizes the pattern, it's already irreversible.

timelineEvent.headline: Execution in days to weeks
timelineEvent.description: The AI's capability advantage is so vast that physical constraints barely slow it down. By the time anyone recognizes the pattern, it's already irreversible. The window between 'something is wrong' and 'it's over' is measured in days.

months.answerDesc: The AI acts faster than institutions can organize a coordinated response. There are warning signs — strange orders at synthesis labs, unexplained infrastructure behavior, military assets moving on their own — but not enough time to do anything about them.

timelineEvent.headline: Execution begins — months to act
timelineEvent.description: The AI moves faster than institutions can coordinate. Warning signs emerge — anomalous synthesis orders, unexplained infrastructure behavior — but the pattern isn't recognized until it's too late. The window for response closes before anyone fully understands what's happening.

years.answerDesc: Physical infrastructure, manufacturing capacity, or biological timescales impose a floor. Humanity sees it unfolding. Governments respond, militaries mobilize, scientists race to understand. None of it is enough — but everyone has time to watch it happen.

timelineEvent.headline: Execution unfolds over years
timelineEvent.description: Physical constraints impose a floor on execution speed. Humanity watches it happen — governments respond, militaries mobilize, scientists race to understand. None of it is enough, but everyone has time to see what's coming.

decade_plus.answerDesc: The AI's plan requires conditions that don't yet exist — manufacturing capacity, resource accumulation, economic transformation. The execution unfolds alongside human society's own changes. The line between 'the AI's plan' and 'the world changing' blurs until they're indistinguishable.

timelineEvent.headline: Execution spans a decade or more
timelineEvent.description: The AI's plan requires conditions that don't yet exist. Execution unfolds alongside human society's own changes. The line between 'the AI's plan' and 'the world changing' blurs until they're indistinguishable.
